name: Australia

visas.name: Skilled Independent Visa (subclass 189)
visas.description: Points-tested permanent residence visa for skilled workers who are not sponsored by an employer or a state/territory government. No job offer required.
requirements: Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) through SkillSelect and receive an invitation
Occupation on the Medium and Long-term Strategic Skills List (MLTSSL)
Skills assessment from the relevant Australian assessing authority
Score at least 65 points on the points test
English: IELTS 6.0 overall (or equivalent)
visas.leadsTo: Australian citizenship after 4 years residency (including 1 year as PR)

visas.name: Temporary Skill Shortage Visa (subclass 482)
visas.description: Employer-sponsored temporary visa for skilled workers filling positions that can't be filled by Australian workers. Comes in short-term (2 years) and medium-term (4 years) streams.
requirements: Sponsorship from an approved Australian Standard Business Sponsor
Occupation on the relevant skilled occupation list
At least 2 years of relevant work experience
English language proficiency (IELTS 5.0 overall minimum)
Skills assessment may be required depending on occupation
visas.leadsTo: Employer Nomination Scheme (subclass 186) for permanent residence

visas.name: Partner Visa (subclasses 309 and 100)
visas.description: For spouses and de facto partners of Australian citizens, permanent residents, or eligible New Zealand citizens. Granted in two stages: temporary then permanent.
requirements: Genuine ongoing relationship with an Australian citizen, PR, or eligible NZ citizen
Sponsor must be 18+ and not have recently sponsored another partner
Health and character requirements for both applicant and sponsor
Marriage certificate or evidence of de facto relationship (12+ months of cohabitation)
visas.processingTime: 18–26 months for temporary (309); ~2 years later for permanent (100)

visas.name: Student Visa (subclass 500)
visas.description: Study full-time at a CRICOS-registered Australian education provider. Includes schools, vocational education, English language courses, and universities.
requirements: Enrolment in a CRICOS-registered course
Satisfy the Genuine Student requirement
English language proficiency (varies by institution)
Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC)
Financial capacity: AUD $24,505/year living costs + tuition fees
visas.maxDuration: Length of course (typically up to 5 years)
visas.leadsTo: Temporary Graduate Visa (subclass 485) for 2–4 years after graduation

visas.name: Working Holiday Visa (subclass 417 / 462)
visas.description: For young people to holiday and work in Australia. Subclass 417 is for eligible passport holders; subclass 462 is the Work and Holiday variant for other eligible countries.
requirements: Aged 18–30 (up to 35 for citizens of some countries)
Citizen of an eligible country
AUD $5,000 in available funds
No dependent children accompanying you
Health and character requirements
visas.maxDuration: 12 months; extendable to a 2nd and 3rd year with specified regional work
visas.leadsTo: Regional work experience can contribute points toward a permanent skilled visa

visas.name: Business Innovation and Investment Visa (subclass 188)
visas.description: For business owners, investors, and entrepreneurs. Multiple streams including Business Innovation, Investor, Significant Investor (AUD $5M), and Entrepreneur.
requirements: Nomination by a state or territory government
Invitation from the Department of Home Affairs
Meet the financial and business requirements of your chosen stream
Significant Investor stream: AUD $5 million in complying investments for 4 years
visas.leadsTo: Business Innovation and Investment (Permanent) visa — subclass 888

citizenship.name: Australian Citizenship
citizenship.requiresResidency: 4 years total residency including 1 year as a permanent resident

benefits.childBenefit: Family Tax Benefit Part A — up to AUD $213.36/fortnight per child (2024)
benefits.healthInsurance: Medicare — free for permanent residents and citizens, covered under bilateral agreements for some nationalities
benefits.unemployment: JobSeeker Payment — up to AUD $762.70/fortnight (2024); waiting periods apply for new PRs

taxNotes: Australia taxes worldwide income for tax residents. First year: you may be a temporary resident, limiting tax on foreign income. A Tax File Number (TFN) is required for employment and banking.
